mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered positive retail-sales trend observations produce 20-day S&P 500 returns no higher than unconditional returns over a rolling 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5%, high short-term discount rates reduce equity valuation multiples and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS-above-5% observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a rising unemployment rate signals labor-market churn and late-cycle margin pressure rather than clean expansion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-up condition is followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional median in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month trend in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO trend observations fail to produce a positive average 60-day S&P 500 excess return versus unconditional returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1300 thousand units indicate weak interest-rate-sensitive demand and weaker construction-linked earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: housing slowdown under tight financial conditions
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1300 triggered observations deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or have no negative return spread versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI combined with a federal funds rate above 5% signals sticky inflation under restrictive policy, keeping discount rates elevated and reducing equity risk appetite over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-up and FEDFUNDS-above-5% interaction has nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month unemployment-rate increase above 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand and recession-risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or by returns statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with a federal funds rate above 5% signals a late-cycle economy where strong demand delays easing and sustains valuation pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises over 2 quarters and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-up and FEDFUNDS-above-5% condition produces 60-day S&P 500 returns above unconditional returns in rolling walk-forward tests.
